behavior_summary: U8P_SPIDER::SPIDER::look: When an actor 'looks' at the spider (Event 0), the spider process emits the literal text "spider " (via Item::bark), sets up implied/process state, and then suspends/returns. This is a simple descriptive/feedback handler for examination.

U8P_SPIDER::SPIDER::gotHit: When the spider receives a hit (Event 6), the handler first checks an internal spider field against a threshold (0x0100) and returns early if the check fails. It then queries Npc::isDead and returns if the spider is dead. If alive and the checks pass, it iterates a collection of items associated with the spider/context, uses Item::getWeightIncludingContents to find 'small' items (weight <= 3) that match an additional filter, computes a randomized damage value (using urandom seeded/scaled by an incoming parameter), and calls Item::receiveHit on those items to apply collateral/randomized damage. This models fragile/small items being damaged when the spider is struck.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven entry: SPIDER::look is invoked by the engine when the player/actor examines the spider; SPIDER::gotHit is invoked on hit events.
Guard checks in gotHit: an internal field threshold check and an Npc::isDead() check cause early returns, preventing further processing in those conditions.

Iterative collateral logic: gotHit loops over an item collection, filters by weight and a secondary flag/id, computes randomized damage from the incoming hit parameter, and calls Item::receiveHit per eligible item.
SPIDER::look performs a single external call (Item::bark) then uses an 'implies' bookkeeping op and suspends — no mutations to global flags observed.
open_questions: What exact field is compared against 0x0100 in SPIDER::gotHit and what gameplay state does that field represent (e.g., armor, shielded flag, stunned state)?
What is the precise source of the item collection iterated in gotHit (spider inventory, equipment slots on the attacker, nearby ground items, or a context-provided list)?
What are the semantic meanings of the two gotHit parameters (BP+0Ah and BP+0Ch) at the API level — attacker id, weapon id, damage amount, hit type, or other metadata?
What does the secondary item filter (id/flag != 1) represent (e.g., unbreakable flag, container vs. content, specific shape exclusion)?

main_function.name: U8P_SPIDER::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the examination/description behavior for the spider: emit the short descriptive string "spider " to the inspecting process and set implied/process state before suspending.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_SPIDER::look) dispatched by the engine when a player/actor inspects or looks at the spider process.

function_map.name: U8P_SPIDER::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_SPIDER::look event handler that causes the spider to 'bark' its description.

function_map.name: U8P_SPIDER::gotHit
function_map.role: Hit/damage event handler that applies checks and propagates randomized collateral damage to small items.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit/display a text string from an item/NPC to a target process/player.

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Query whether the NPC (spider) is dead to short-circuit hit handling.

function_map.name: Item::getWeightIncludingContents
function_map.role: Return an item's weight (including contained items) used to identify small/fragile items.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Provide a random value used to scale randomized damage applied to items.

function_map.name: Item::receiveHit
function_map.role: Apply damage/hit effects to an item instance (damage magnitude/type/etc.).

external_calls.purpose: Make the spider emit the literal string "spider " to the inspecting process/player.
Check whether the spider is dead and skip damage propagation if so.
Measure each candidate item's weight (including contents) to filter 'small' items eligible for collateral damage.
Generate randomness scaled by an incoming hit parameter to vary damage applied to items.
Apply calculated/randomized damage to individual items selected by the filter.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_SPIDER::SPIDER::look: When an actor 'looks' at the spider (Event 0), the spider process emits the literal text "spider " (via Item::bark), sets up implied/process state, and then suspends/returns. This is a simple descriptive/feedback handler for examination.
